event: Treated with aspirin (enteric coated tab, 100 mg)
Aspirin was stopped 7 d before surgery
No medication was administered on the morning of surgery
Prior to surgery, chest X-ray was normal
Echocardiogram showed left ventricular ejection fraction of 61%
Total knee arthroplasty (TKA) under spinal anesthesia
Bupivacaine (0.5%, 10 mg) injected into subarachnoid space at T10 for spinal block
Pneumatic thigh tourniquet applied at 300 mmHg for 100 min
Total anesthesia time was 2 h and 10 min
600 mL of crystalloid administered during surgery
150 mL of urine collected during surgery
On arrival in recovery room BP 97/55 mmHg
On arrival in recovery room HR 41 beats/min
On arrival in recovery room SpO2 100%
400 mL of crystalloid administered in recovery room
Post-operative pain managed with intravenous patient-controlled analgesia including fentanyl
Morning following surgery complained of severe pain on the right thigh
Tramadol hydrochloride injected for pain control
Exhibited a drowsy mental state about 24 h after TKA
Right thigh was stiff and had turned a dark brown
Surgical site and distal extremity were unaffected
Urine in Foley bag was dark colored
Urine output every 8 h after operation was 100 mL
Urine output every 8 h after operation was 220 mL
Urine output every 8 h after operation was 250 mL
About 19 h after surgery, CK increased to 2763 IU/L
Thrombocytopenia detected in early postoperative phase
Progressive deterioration of disseminated intravascular coagulation (DIC)
Brain MRI performed; no notable hemorrhage or infarction
Random urine analysis RBC 100/high powered field
About 1 h after mental status change, BP dropped to 88/62 mmHg
Arterial line placed in left radial artery
Central line secured in right internal jugular vein
Dialysis scheduled due to progressive renal impairment
Fluid loading initiated with normal saline
Sodium bicarbonate administered for urine alkalization
Mental status restored to alert about 2 h after fluid administration
